deep_dive.title: Unprivileged Topology Certificates for Cloud GPU Attestation
deep_dive.abstract: Cloud GPU tenants receive a model name and a region, but cannot directly inspect the physical accelerator that runs their job. We present a software-only attestation primitive for this setting. A CUDA probe measures an SM-by-memory-region latency matrix using physical SM labels and dependent global loads. A streaming reducer commits sufficient statistics, configuration, code hashes, network evidence, and a compressed raw data archive into a certificate that a verifier can check without a GPU. The certificate supports three claims. First, the per-SM latency map is a stable physical fingerprint. Over a six-hour full-load RTX 5090 run, its median temporal jitter is 0.09 cycles, while shape-only leave-one-out classification separates distinct Blackwell dies with 100.0% accuracy. Second, cache-bypassing HBM sweeps recover hardware-class topology across generations, including a unified Volta V100 memory domain, a two-way Hopper H200 L2 split, and a Blackwell B200 two-die NV-HBI package whose 74/74 SM partition carries a 30-cycle, 15.5 ns cross-die penalty. Third, public network landmarks bind the same certificate to a coarse location. In the B200 run, 169 RIPE Atlas probes place the server within 44 km of its claimed datacentre and reject all 11 decoy sites. Together, these measurements check cloud-GPU identity, class, and coarse location without privileged access or a vendor key.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Proposes a software-only GPU attestation primitive using latency-based topology fingerprinting, enabling cloud tenants to verify physical accelerators.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出基于延迟拓扑指纹的纯软件GPU证明原语，使云租户能验证物理加速器。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出基于延迟拓扑指纹的纯软件GPU证明原语，使云租户能验证物理加速器。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出基于延迟拓扑指纹的纯软件GPU证明原语，使云租户能验证物理加速器。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper presents a CUDA-based probe that measures an SM-by-memory-region latency matrix to create a stable physical fingerprint of cloud GPUs. The certificate supports GPU identification and tamper detection, providing a non-privileged attestation method for cloud tenants, enhancing trust in heterogeneous computing environments.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出一个基于CUDA的探针，测量流多处理器对内存区域的延迟矩阵，形成稳定的GPU物理指纹，并生成可验证证书。该非特权证明方法使云租户能够验证其使用的物理GPU是否被篡改或误标，增强了异构计算环境中对云端加速器的信任。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文提出一个基于CUDA的探针，测量流多处理器对内存区域的延迟矩阵，形成稳定的GPU物理指纹，并生成可验证证书。该非特权证明方法使云租户能够验证其使用的物理GPU是否被篡改或误标，增强了异构计算环境中对云端加速器的信任。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Security challenges in heterogeneous systems with CPUs and GPUs

deep_dive.title: Kops: Safely Extending the eBPF Compilation Pipeline with Native Operations
deep_dive.abstract: eBPF safely extends OS kernels in domains such as networking, observability, and security. The safety comes from an in-kernel compilation pipeline where a verifier checks every program, and a kernel just-in-time compiler (JIT) translates the verified bytecode to native code. The kernel keeps the JIT simple to stay trustworthy, translating one bytecode instruction at a time in a single pass. This single-pass design misses optimization opportunities, so eBPF runs up to twice as slow as natively compiled code in our characterization. Adding optimizations to the kernel JIT directly requires upstream acceptance and a long release cycle, enlarges the trusted computing base (TCB), and grows the per-architecture kernel code. To address this, we present Kops, an extension interface that lets userspace compilers and kernel modules introduce new operations without modifying the kernel core, while keeping a minimal trusted computing base (TCB). Each operation has two forms, a proof sequence of vanilla eBPF instructions that the existing verifier checks and a native emit of machine instructions that the JIT compiles. Because the verifier checks the proof sequence, the native emit is the only per-operation addition to the TCB. Hardware idioms are the lowest-hanging fruit for this interface. With Kops, we build EInsn, seven operations such as rotate and conditional select that CPUs execute as single instructions. Lean 4 proofs show that each native emit computes the same result as its proof sequence. On x86-64 and ARM64, EInsn speeds up eBPF microbenchmarks by up to 24% and production applications by up to 12%. The same interface also supports whole-program native replacement, reaching 2.358x at the cost of a larger TCB.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Safely extending eBPF with native operations for kernel defense
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过原生操作安全扩展eBPF以增强内核防御
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过原生操作安全扩展eBPF以增强内核防御
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过原生操作安全扩展eBPF以增强内核防御
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Kops enables safe native operations in eBPF, overcoming single-pass JIT overhead, and achieves performance gains while preserving kernel safety.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对eBPF内核JIT单次编译导致程序运行速度慢、优化困难的问题，提出Kops框架，允许将原生代码片段安全嵌入eBPF程序并利用验证器保证安全性，实验表明性能提升接近原生编译，同时不增加内核可信计算基，拓展了高性能安全内核扩展的可行性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对eBPF内核JIT单次编译导致程序运行速度慢、优化困难的问题，提出Kops框架，允许将原生代码片段安全嵌入eBPF程序并利用验证器保证安全性，实验表明性能提升接近原生编译，同时不增加内核可信计算基，拓展了高性能安全内核扩展的可行性。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: defense mechanisms for operating system kernels
